post.description:
Create a new complex order (conditional or iceberg).

Provide exactly one of `conditional_order_params` (TP/SL/STOP) or
`iceberg_order_params`. The body shape determines the complex_order_type.

Returns 202 Accepted with the persisted complex order in PENDING status,
enqueued for activation by the appropriate consumer service.

Cancel all cancellable complex orders (conditional and iceberg) for a subaccount.

Returns the list of complex_order_ids that were submitted for cancellation.

delete.description:
Cancel a single complex order (conditional or iceberg).

Returns 202 Accepted. Cancellation is processed asynchronously by the
appropriate consumer service.

get.description:
Search and browse markets across all exchanges.

Supports:
- Exact match on ticker, condition_id, slug, river_id
- Full-text search on name, description, tags
- Browse by filters (no query required)
- Results ranked by relevance

We default to active markets in the view so things are faster

get.description: Batch lookup markets by river_ids.
Exact match a single market by Kalshi ticker or Polymarket slug. Supply exactly one.

post.description:
Batch-resolve many markets by exact Kalshi ticker and/or Polymarket slug in one request.

The single-market ``/match`` endpoint costs one round-trip per identifier, so resolving
thousands of mappings serially is slow. This endpoint takes lists of tickers and/or slugs
(send them together in one call) and returns a full market row — including ``river_id``,
``ticker``, ``slug`` and ``exchange_name`` — for every identifier that matched. Identifiers
with no match come back in ``unmatched_tickers`` / ``unmatched_slugs``.

get.description: Get orderbook for a river_id.

parameters.name: levels

schema.description: Number of price levels to return
schema.title: Levels

parameters.description: Number of price levels to return

post.description:
Place a new order.

Submits an order for asynchronous processing. The order is persisted immediately
and queued for execution on the target exchange.

See  [Orders](https://docs.rivermarkets.com/concepts/orders) and [Order Types](https://docs.rivermarkets.com/concepts/order-types) for more information.

**Asset Selection:**
Provide exactly one of `river_id` (standard instrument) or `generic_asset_id` (user-defined basket).
Generic asset orders only support `MARKET` order type — the system automatically routes
IOC limit orders to each underlying instrument at optimal prices.

**Response:**
Returns `202 Accepted` with the order_id and associated complex_orders ids (TP/SL).
Poll `GET /v1/orders/{order_id}` or use webhooks to track execution.

get.description:
List orders with optional filters.

Returns a paginated list of orders across all accessible subaccounts, ordered by `created_at` descending.

**Filters:**
All filters are optional and can be combined. If no `subaccount_id` is specified,
returns orders from all subaccounts the user has access to.

**Order Statuses:**
- `PENDING_SUBMISSION`: Order received, awaiting exchange submission.
- `PROCESSING`: Being processed by the order consumer.
- `RESTING`: Live on the exchange order book (may include partially-filled live orders).
- `EXECUTED`: Terminal — fully filled.
- `PARTIALLY_FILLED`: Terminal — cancelled with some quantity filled.
- `CANCELLED`: Terminal — cancelled with no fills.
- `REJECTED`: Rejected by exchange (see order details for reason).

**Aggregated Fields:**
Each order includes `traded_qty`, `average_price`, and `fees_paid` computed from fills.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by order status. Accepts a single OrderStatus name (e.g. 'RESTING') or the meta-value 'ACTIVE' which resolves to RESTING.
schema.title: Status

Applied server-side over all matching orders, not just the current page.

Applied server-side over all matching orders, not just the current page.

get.description:
Get a single order by ID with its fills.

Returns complete order details including execution history.

**Use Cases:**
- Track order progress and execution quality
- Reconcile fills for accounting
- Debug rejected or partially filled orders

delete.description:
Cancel an existing order.

Request cancellation of an order.
Returns 202 Accepted with order state - cancellation is processed asynchronously.

patch.description:
Edit (amend) a resting simple limit order.

Only `qty` and `price` are editable. `qty` is the new TOTAL quantity (not remaining).
The order must be RESTING, a simple order (no iceberg/peg parent), a single-instrument
LIMIT order. Returns 202 Accepted; final status is delivered via the orders WS.

get.description:
Get the best (minimum) queue position across all exchange orders backing this river order.

Currently supported only for Kalshi-backed orders.

post.description:
Cancel all open orders for a subaccount.

If `river_ids` is set, only orders on those instruments are cancelled;
it must be omitted/null or non-empty. Cancellation is processed
asynchronously by the order consumer.

get.description:
Fetch positions for user's subaccounts.

Positions are computed from cumulative fills, with avg buy/sell prices,
fees, and fill count. Returns positions grouped by subaccount.
By default, only positions in open markets are returned.

Get price data for a market.
Returns either candlestick OHLC data or simple price history based on the `type` parameter.
The endpoint automatically determines the exchange and fetches data accordingly.

- **Kalshi**: Returns real OHLC candlestick data with volume
- **Polymarket**: Returns price history (candlesticks will have synthetic OHLC where all values equal the price)

All prices are normalized to 0-1 range.

parameters.name: fidelity

schema.description: Minutes per data point (for Polymarket history)
schema.title: Fidelity

parameters.description: Minutes per data point (for Polymarket history)

get.description:
Enumerate all trading subaccounts accessible to the user.

Returns list of accounts with identifiers and metadata.

schema.title: Response List Subaccounts V1 Subaccounts Get

post.description: Create a new trading subaccount for the authenticated user.

get.description:
Fetch configuration and metadata for a specific subaccount.

Returns account configuration, limits, and descriptive metadata.

delete.description:
Delete a subaccount. Custodial subaccounts cannot be deleted.

Refused with 409 if the subaccount has any open order (resting/in-flight
orders, or an active iceberg, peg, smart-taker or conditional order) —
cancel those first.

This is a soft delete: the row is marked is_active = false so the audit trail
(orders, fills, ledger entries) stays intact. The user never sees it again.

get.description:
Return the most recent trades for one or more markets, sourced directly from
the exchanges. Markets are fetched concurrently with a small semaphore
(`MAX_CONCURRENT_FETCHES`) so a long input list never fans out into a
thundering herd, and the result list preserves the input order. A failure
on any single market becomes a `not_found` row with a message — the call as
a whole does not 500.

Per-market lookup paths:
  - Kalshi: `GET /markets/trades?ticker=...&limit=...`
  - Polymarket: `GET https://data-api.polymarket.com/trades?market=<token>&limit=...`
    (both YES and NO legs are fetched and the NO leg is flipped into YES-price terms)

Trades are sorted newest-first within each market. Prices are normalised to 0-1.

Use this together with the `/v1/ws/tradeprints` WebSocket: hit this endpoint to
backfill the tape on page load, then dedup live WS events by `exchange_trade_id`.

conditional_order_type.description: Type of conditional order: TP (take profit), SL (stop loss), or STOP

stop_order_price.title: Stop Order Price
stop_order_price.description: Activation price for STOP conditional orders. This is the price at which the conditional order triggers and places the trigger_order.

trigger_order.description: The order that gets placed when the conditional order activates. For TP/SL this happens after the parent order fills; for STOP this happens when stop_order_price is reached.

conditional_order_type.title: Conditional Order Type
conditional_order_type.description: Type of conditional order: TP (take profit), SL (stop loss), or STOP

stop_order_price.title: Stop Order Price
stop_order_price.description: Activation price for STOP conditional orders. The price at which the conditional triggers and places the trigger order.

trigger_stop_order_price.title: Trigger Stop Order Price
trigger_stop_order_price.description: Stop/reverse-stop price for the triggered order (SL or TP MARKET)

trigger_order_type.description: Order type of the triggered order: LIMIT or MARKET

trigger_order_limit_price.title: Trigger Order Limit Price
trigger_order_limit_price.description: Limit price of the triggered order (if LIMIT)

trigger_complex_order_id.title: Trigger Complex Order Id
trigger_complex_order_id.description: Complex order ID of the triggered order (set once activated)

ConditionalOrderResponse.title: ConditionalOrderResponse
ConditionalOrderResponse.description:
Response for a conditional order. Contains both the condition definition and the trigger order
that gets placed when the condition is met.

For TP/SL: the parent order fills → the conditional activates → the trigger order is placed.
For STOP/REVERSE_STOP: stop_order_price is reached → the trigger order is placed.

displayed_qty.title: Displayed Qty
displayed_qty.description: Tranche size — quantity shown to the market at any moment

limit_price.title: Limit Price
limit_price.description: Static limit price for every tranche (between 0 and 1)

post_only.title: Post Only
post_only.description: If true, every tranche is posted as post-only (maker-only)

cancel_order_on_pause.title: Cancel Order On Pause
cancel_order_on_pause.description: Kalshi only: if true, resting tranches are cancelled by the exchange when it enters a trading pause. Ignored on other venues.

reload_delay_s.title: Reload Delay S
reload_delay_s.description: Optional delay between a tranche fully filling and the next one being placed, in seconds. NULL or 0 = place next tranche immediately.

expiry_ts_utc.title: Expiry Ts Utc
expiry_ts_utc.description: Optional expiry timestamp in UTC (ISO 8601). When set, every tranche is submitted to the venue as GTD with this expiry; venue-side expiry of a tranche cascades to cancel the parent iceberg.

IcebergOrderParams.title: IcebergOrderParams
IcebergOrderParams.description:
Parameters for creating an iceberg complex order.

An iceberg's `total_qty` is split into sequential `displayed_qty` tranches
posted at the same `limit_price`. When a tranche fully fills, the next is
placed automatically until the total is exhausted or the iceberg is cancelled.

limit_price.title: Limit Price
limit_price.description: Static limit price for every tranche

post_only.title: Post Only
post_only.description: Whether tranches are posted as post-only

cancel_order_on_pause.title: Cancel Order On Pause
cancel_order_on_pause.description: Kalshi only: whether the exchange cancels resting tranches during a trading pause

reload_delay_s.title: Reload Delay S
reload_delay_s.description: Inter-tranche reload delay in seconds, if set. NULL or 0 = place next tranche immediately.

executed_qty.title: Executed Qty
executed_qty.description: Sum of fully-executed child tranches' qty

expiry_ts_utc.title: Expiry Ts Utc
expiry_ts_utc.description: Expiry timestamp in UTC, if set. Tranches are submitted as GTD with this expiry.

IcebergOrderResponse.title: IcebergOrderResponse
IcebergOrderResponse.description:
Iceberg-specific runtime detail returned alongside a ComplexOrderResponse.

Sourced from `view_iceberg_orders` so it includes derived fields
(executed_qty, remaining_qty).

InstrumentStatus.title: InstrumentStatus
InstrumentStatus.description:
Instrument status derived in instruments table from exchange-specific fields.

Kalshi: maps from kalshi_markets.status
    - 'active' -> ACTIVE
    - everything else (closed, determined, finalized, settled, amended, inactive, initialized) -> CLOSED

Polymarket: maps from polymarket_markets.closed and polymarket_markets.active booleans
    - closed=true -> CLOSED
    - active=true (and not closed) -> ACTIVE
    - else (active=false, closed=false) -> INACTIVE

MarketLookupResponse.title: MarketLookupResponse
MarketLookupResponse.description: Schema for batch market lookup response.

tickers.title: Tickers
tickers.description: Exact Kalshi tickers to resolve (e.g. KXNBA-26-DET).

slugs.title: Slugs
slugs.description: Exact Polymarket / Polymarket-US slugs to resolve (e.g. will-the-denver-nuggets-win-the-2026-nba-finals).

MarketMatchBatchRequest.title: MarketMatchBatchRequest
MarketMatchBatchRequest.description: Batch exact-match request: resolve many Kalshi tickers / Polymarket slugs at once.

MarketMatchBatchResponse.title: MarketMatchBatchResponse
MarketMatchBatchResponse.description:
Batch exact-match response.

``results`` holds a full market row (with ``river_id``, ``ticker``, ``slug`` and
``exchange_name``) for every identifier that matched. Identifiers with no match are
echoed back in ``unmatched_tickers`` / ``unmatched_slugs`` so callers can tell the
difference between "not found" and a lookup they forgot to send.

start_datetime.title: Start Datetime
start_datetime.description: Game/event start datetime (UTC). Populated for sports; null otherwise.

last_price.title: Last Price
last_price.description: Most recent YES price normalized to 0.0–1.0 (probability). Kalshi cents / 100; Polymarket already 0.0–1.0. NULL when no trade has been observed.

primary_entity_name.title: Primary Entity Name
primary_entity_name.description: Resolved display name of the canonical structured-target entity (player, team, competitor, …) referenced by the market. NULL for markets without a structured target. Kalshi only — Polymarket has no equivalent concept.

MarketSearchResult.title: MarketSearchResult
MarketSearchResult.description: Schema for a single market search result.

price.title: Price
price.description: Limit price between 0 and 1. Required for LIMIT orders.

post_only.title: Post Only
post_only.description: If true, the order is rejected instead of taking liquidity. Only valid for LIMIT orders with a resting time in force (GTC or GTD).

cancel_order_on_pause.title: Cancel Order On Pause
cancel_order_on_pause.description: Kalshi only: if true, the resting order is cancelled by the exchange when it enters a trading pause. Ignored on other venues.

river_id.description: Instrument ID. Mutually exclusive with generic_asset_id.

conditional_orders_params.description: Optional list of conditional orders (TP/SL) to attach to this order.

cancel_order_on_pause.title: Cancel Order On Pause
cancel_order_on_pause.description: Kalshi only: whether the exchange cancels the resting order during a trading pause

status.description: Order status. Live: PENDING_SUBMISSION, PROCESSING, RESTING. Terminal: EXECUTED, PARTIALLY_FILLED (cancelled with some fills), CANCELLED, REJECTED.

reject_reason.description: Reason for rejection (if status is REJECTED)

parent_iceberg_order_id.title: Parent Iceberg Order Id
parent_iceberg_order_id.description: If this order is a tranche of an iceberg parent, the parent's id; otherwise null.

parent_peg_order_id.title: Parent Peg Order Id
parent_peg_order_id.description: If this order is the resting child of a peg parent, the parent's id; otherwise null.

parent_smart_taker_order_id.title: Parent Smart Taker Order Id
parent_smart_taker_order_id.description: If this order is an IOC child of a smart-taker parent, the parent's id; otherwise null.

fills.title: Fills
fills.description: List of executions (fills) for this order

OrderDetailResponse.title: OrderDetailResponse
OrderDetailResponse.description: Schema for order detail response including fills.

OrderEditRequest.title: OrderEditRequest
OrderEditRequest.description:
Schema for editing (amending) an existing simple limit order.

`qty` is the TOTAL order quantity (not remaining) — matches Kalshi's amend semantics.
At least one of `qty` or `price` must be provided.

status.title: Status
status.description: Always PENDING_AMEND. Final status (RESTING on success, REJECTED on failure) is delivered via the orders WS.

fee.title: Fee
fee.description: Fee charged by the exchange for this fill

is_maker.title: Is Maker
is_maker.description: Whether this fill provided liquidity (maker) or took it (taker)

OrderFillResponse.title: OrderFillResponse
OrderFillResponse.description: Schema for a single fill on an order.

orders.title: Orders
orders.description: List of orders matching the query

count.title: Count
count.description: Total count of matching orders (for pagination)

exchange_order_id.title: Exchange Order Id
exchange_order_id.description: Exchange order ID with the best (minimum) queue priority

queue_position.title: Queue Position
queue_position.description: Number of preceding shares in the queue ahead of this order (lower is better priority)

OrderQueuePositionResponse.title: OrderQueuePositionResponse
OrderQueuePositionResponse.description: Schema for the queue-position response of an order.

time_in_force.title: Time In Force
time_in_force.description: Time in force: FOK, GTC, GTD, or IOC

cancel_order_on_pause.title: Cancel Order On Pause
cancel_order_on_pause.description: Kalshi only: whether the exchange cancels the resting order during a trading pause

status.title: Status
status.description: Order status. Live: PENDING_SUBMISSION, PROCESSING, RESTING. Terminal: EXECUTED, PARTIALLY_FILLED (cancelled with some fills), CANCELLED, REJECTED.

expiry_ts_utc.title: Expiry Ts Utc
expiry_ts_utc.description: Expiry timestamp in UTC for GTD orders

fees_paid.title: Fees Paid
fees_paid.description: Total fees paid across all fills

complex_order_ids.title: Complex Order Ids
complex_order_ids.description: List of complex river orders id

reject_reason.title: Reject Reason
reject_reason.description: Reason for rejection (if status is REJECTED)

parent_iceberg_order_id.title: Parent Iceberg Order Id
parent_iceberg_order_id.description: If this order is a tranche of an iceberg parent, the parent's id; otherwise null.

parent_peg_order_id.title: Parent Peg Order Id
parent_peg_order_id.description: If this order is the resting child of a peg parent, the parent's id; otherwise null.

parent_smart_taker_order_id.title: Parent Smart Taker Order Id
parent_smart_taker_order_id.description: If this order is an IOC child of a smart-taker parent, the parent's id; otherwise null.

OrderResponse.title: OrderResponse
OrderResponse.description: Schema for order response, when user request information about an order.

min_price.title: Min Price
min_price.description: Price floor — the child never rests below this (between 0 and 1)

max_price.title: Max Price
max_price.description: Price ceiling — the child never rests above this (between 0 and 1)

post_only.title: Post Only
post_only.description: If true, the resting child order is posted as post-only; a repeg that would cross the book cancels the peg instead of taking liquidity

expiry_ts_utc.title: Expiry Ts Utc
expiry_ts_utc.description: Optional expiry timestamp in UTC (ISO 8601). When set, the resting child is submitted to the venue as GTD with this expiry; venue-side expiry cancels the peg (caught by the periodic reconciliation sweep).

peg_min_stay_time_s.title: Peg Min Stay Time S
peg_min_stay_time_s.description: Optional repeg gate. When set, a price-driven repeg is suppressed until the resting child has been on the book for at least this many seconds (measured from send time). Expiry-driven cancels are unaffected.

max_qty_level.title: Max Qty Level
max_qty_level.description: Penny-jump threshold (contracts). If the level the peg would join holds more than this many contracts, rest one tick tighter instead (if not crossing the book and not already quoting the level). Unset to disable.

PegOrderParams.title: PegOrderParams
PegOrderParams.description:
Parameters for creating a peg complex order.

A peg's child limit order rests on the venue at the current best bid (for buys)
or best ask (for sells), clamped to the absolute band [min_price, max_price]:
the child never rests below min_price or above max_price. Both bounds are
optional — an unset min floors at 0, an unset max ceils at 1. The peg worker
repegs the child as the top of book moves.

min_price.title: Min Price
min_price.description: Price floor — the child never rests below this

max_price.title: Max Price
max_price.description: Price ceiling — the child never rests above this

post_only.title: Post Only
post_only.description: Whether the resting child order is post-only

expiry_ts_utc.title: Expiry Ts Utc
expiry_ts_utc.description: Expiry timestamp in UTC, if set. The child is submitted as GTD with this expiry.

peg_min_stay_time_s.title: Peg Min Stay Time S
peg_min_stay_time_s.description: Minimum seconds a resting child must stay on the book before a price-driven repeg is allowed, if set.

max_qty_level.title: Max Qty Level
max_qty_level.description: Penny-jump threshold (contracts): levels larger than this are stepped past, one tick inside the spread.

PegOrderResponse.title: PegOrderResponse
PegOrderResponse.description:
Peg-specific runtime detail returned alongside a ComplexOrderResponse.

Sourced from `view_peg_orders` so it includes derived fields
(executed_qty, remaining_qty).

PositionResponse.title: PositionResponse
PositionResponse.description: Schema for a position (computed from cumulative fills).

PricePoint.title: PricePoint
PricePoint.description: Simple price point for history type.

total_qty.title: Total Qty
total_qty.description: Total quantity to acquire/offload across all IOC clips

limit_price.title: Limit Price
limit_price.description: Worst price the taker will accept — max for buy, min for sell (between 0 and 1)

min_qty.title: Min Qty
min_qty.description: Book-depth trigger gate: only fire when at least this much acceptable liquidity sits within limit_price on the opposite side. A depth floor, not a per-clip fill floor — it may exceed total_qty (e.g. 'take my 100 only when >=500 is resting') and an IOC may fill less if liquidity vanishes mid-flight.

expiry_ts_utc.title: Expiry Ts Utc
expiry_ts_utc.description: Optional expiry timestamp in UTC (ISO 8601). Once it passes, the order stops firing and is torn down by the periodic reconciliation sweep.

SmartTakerOrderParams.title: SmartTakerOrderParams
SmartTakerOrderParams.description:
Parameters for creating a smart-taker complex order.

A smart-taker rests nothing on the book. It watches the opposite side and
fires an IOC sweep (at `limit_price`, for the full remaining qty) whenever at
least `min_qty` of acceptable liquidity (price within `limit_price`) is
resting on the book — amortising taker fee/spread and refusing to cross into
a thin book.

total_qty.title: Total Qty
total_qty.description: Total quantity across all IOC clips

limit_price.title: Limit Price
limit_price.description: Worst price the taker will accept

min_qty.title: Min Qty
min_qty.description: Book-depth trigger gate (min acceptable liquidity resting before firing)

expiry_ts_utc.title: Expiry Ts Utc
expiry_ts_utc.description: Expiry timestamp in UTC, if set.

SmartTakerOrderResponse.title: SmartTakerOrderResponse
SmartTakerOrderResponse.description:
Smart-taker-specific runtime detail returned alongside a ComplexOrderResponse.

Sourced from `view_smart_taker_orders` so it includes derived fields
(executed_qty, remaining_qty).

SubaccountCreate.title: SubaccountCreate
SubaccountCreate.description: Schema for creating a new subaccount.

exchange_trade_id.title: Exchange Trade Id
exchange_trade_id.description: Exchange-assigned trade id. Used by clients to dedup overlap between REST backfill and WS live stream.

TradeprintEvent.title: TradeprintEvent
TradeprintEvent.description:
A single trade print.

`aggressor_buy_flag` is true when the aggressing (taker) side bought —
i.e. the trade hit an ask. The frontend renders side color and "Buy/Sell"
from this flag.

TradeprintsBulkResponse.title: TradeprintsBulkResponse
TradeprintsBulkResponse.description:
Response for `GET /v1/tradeprints?river_ids=...`.

`results` preserves the order of the requested `river_ids`. Partial failures
are surfaced per-row (status `not_found`) with a `message` — a bad river_id
or upstream error never aborts the whole call.

TradeprintsResponse.title: TradeprintsResponse
TradeprintsResponse.description: Per-market block of recent trades from the exchange.

order_type.description: Order type for the triggered order: 'LIMIT' or 'MARKET'

qty.title: Qty
qty.description: Quantity (number of contracts) for the triggered order

price.title: Price
price.description: Limit price for the triggered order (between 0 and 1). Required when order_type is LIMIT.

buy_flag.title: Buy Flag
buy_flag.description: Direction of the triggered order: true=buy, false=sell

stop_order_price.title: Stop Order Price
stop_order_price.description: Price at which the triggered order activates as a stop or reverse stop. Required for SL (stop trigger) and TP MARKET (reverse stop trigger). For SL: triggers when price moves against you. For TP MARKET: triggers when price moves in your favor.

TriggerOrder.title: TriggerOrder
TriggerOrder.description:
The order that will be sent to the exchange when the conditional order's condition is met.

For TP/SL: the parent order fills → the conditional activates → this trigger order is placed.
For STOP/REVERSE_STOP: the stop_order_price is reached → this trigger order is placed.

UserFillListResponse.title: UserFillListResponse
UserFillListResponse.description: Schema for list of fills for a user.

WatchlistCreate.title: WatchlistCreate
WatchlistCreate.description: Request schema for creating a new watchlist.

WatchlistDetailResponse.title: WatchlistDetailResponse
WatchlistDetailResponse.description:
Response schema for a watchlist with its resolved members (detail view).

``members`` are ordered by ``position``; ``river_ids`` is the same ordering
as a convenience for handing straight to the multibook handoff.

WatchlistMemberAdd.title: WatchlistMemberAdd
WatchlistMemberAdd.description: Request schema for adding members to a watchlist (appended at the end).

WatchlistMemberReorder.title: WatchlistMemberReorder
WatchlistMemberReorder.description:
Request schema for reordering a watchlist.

The body is the full ordered list of ``river_id``s; the server rewrites
``position`` to match. Sending the whole array avoids partial-reorder races
and keeps it to one round-trip.

WatchlistResponse.title: WatchlistResponse
WatchlistResponse.description: A watchlist summary (list view): metadata + ordered member river_ids.

WatchlistUpdate.title: WatchlistUpdate
WatchlistUpdate.description: Request schema for renaming / editing a watchlist.

tags.name: markets
tags.description: Search and discover prediction markets across Kalshi and Polymarket.

tags.name: subaccounts
tags.description: Manage trading subaccounts. Each subaccount can have its own exchange credentials and positions.

tags.name: positions
tags.description: View current portfolio positions across all connected exchanges.

tags.name: fills
tags.description: Retrieve trade execution history and fill details.

tags.name: prices
tags.description: Get historical price data and candlesticks from exchanges.

tags.name: tradeprints
tags.description: Recent trades for a market. Pairs with the /v1/ws/tradeprints WebSocket for live updates.

tags.name: orders
tags.description: Place and manage orders on prediction market exchanges.

tags.name: complex-orders
tags.description: Manage complex orders: conditional orders (take-profit and stop-loss), TWAP, and other advanced order types.
